mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points within three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in over 65% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment remains below 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates robust aggregate demand growth, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline, or 60-day forward returns turn negative when both series grow above their 5-year moving averages.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in the industrial production index reflects immediate supply-side bottlenecks and factory slowdowns, triggering short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in 70% of contraction episodes, or the signal shows zero predictive accuracy during periods of stable industrial output.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with a stable or rising federal funds rate indicates real tightening, which increases discount rates and compresses equity valuations, leading to negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally despite rising real rates, or the interaction term yields a negative Rank IC over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls provides a reliable leading indicator of economic resilience and wage-driven consumption, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of months with positive payroll growth, or the correlation between payroll changes and equity returns drops below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden drop in housing starts below 1.1 million units signals a sharp pullback in construction demand and related supply chains, causing immediate negative sentiment and S&P 500 declines over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 5-day returns in over 60% of instances when housing starts breach the threshold, or the signal loses significance during housing market upswings.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling unemployment indicates an overheating economy where supply constraints outpace labor absorption, prompting market repricing and negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative returns, or 20-day forward returns remain positive when industrial output accelerates alongside falling unemployment.
